title: Pearson Edexcel International GCSE Biology Paper 2B - November 2025
subject: Biology

sub_questions.ms_text: An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. (increased) temperature / light (1). 2. (increased) rate of photosynthesis (1). 3. (so more) oxygen produced (1).
Correct Answer: B (P and R). Q is incorrect as it is respiration. S is incorrect as it is combustion.
An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. jar is closed / sealed (1). 2. carbon dioxide is recycled / reused (1). 3. plants respire AND photosynthesise (1).
Any two from: 1. reduce use of fossil fuels (1). 2. use renewable energy / nuclear (1). 3. plant more trees / reduce deforestation (1). 4. reduce livestock farming (1).

An explanation that makes reference to four: 1. greenhouse gases trap heat / reflect IR (1). 2. global warming / earth temperature increases (1). 3. ice caps melt / sea levels rise (1). 4. habitat loss / loss of biodiversity (1). 5. extreme weather / migration (1).
An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. sulfur dioxide dissolves in water (in clouds) (1). 2. forms sulfuric acid / acid rain (1). 3. damages trees / kills fish / lowers pH of lakes (1).
lichens (1).
An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. (less) nitrogen oxides / soot (1). 2. reduces acid rain (1). 3. improves air quality / reduces smog (1).
A description that makes reference to: 1. CO binds to haemoglobin (1). 2. reduces oxygen-carrying capacity of blood (1).

A description that makes reference to four: 1. use quadrats (1). 2. random sampling / random coordinates (1). 3. count number of species / named species (1). 4. repeat at different heights / areas (1). 5. calculate mean per quadrat (1).

An answer that makes reference to four: 1. light intensity increases with height (1). 2. temperature increases with height (1). 3. more light/warmth for photosynthesis (1). 4. more minerals/water at base (1). 5. competition for space (1).
Correct Answer: C (oestrogen). A is incorrect as adrenaline is for fight/flight. B is incorrect as insulin is for glucose. D is incorrect as testosterone is male.
An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. thickening of uterus lining (1). 2. maintenance of lining (1). 3. inhibits FSH / LH (1).
An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. corpus luteum produces progesterone (1). 2. lining remains thick for implantation (1). 3. if no pregnancy, progesterone falls and lining sheds (1).
An explanation that makes reference to: 1. placenta develops (1). 2. diffusion of glucose/oxygen to foetus (1). 3. diffusion of CO2/urea from foetus (1). 4. umbilical cord transports blood (1).
Correct Answer: D (X, Y and Z). All three genotypes (AA, Aa, aa) result from a cross of two heterozygotes.
An answer that makes reference to two: 1. parents are Aa and Aa (1). 2. offspring genotypes are AA, Aa, Aa, aa (1). 3. ratio 3:1 (1).
A description that makes reference to two: 1. cystic fibrosis (1). 2. mucus in lungs / digestive system (1). 3. difficulty breathing / absorbing nutrients (1).
A description that makes reference to three: 1. gene therapy (1). 2. use of vector / virus / inhaler (1). 3. insert working allele into cells (1). 4. cells produce correct protein (1).
9.4 (2 marks). (Calculation: 15 / 1.6). Accept 9. Allow 1 mark for correct working.
An answer that makes reference to: 1. antibodies produced (1). 2. memory cells made (1). 3. secondary response faster/higher (1).
An answer that makes reference to two: 1. no need for needles / cheaper (1). 2. easier to administer (1). 3. herd immunity (1).
antigen (1).
An answer that makes reference to: 1. small sample size (1). 2. health / age / sex not known (1). 3. only one virus tested (1). 4. no control group mentioned (1).
lymphocyte / B-cell (1).

An answer that makes reference to four: 1. transcription in nucleus (1). 2. translation at ribosomes (1). 3. mRNA copied from DNA (1). 4. tRNA brings amino acids (1). 5. codons bind to anticodons (1). 6. polypeptide chain made (1).
1150 (%) (3 marks). (Calculation: (25 - 2) / 2 * 100). Allow 1 mark for 23, 2 marks for 23/2.

An answer that makes reference to five: 1. restrict movement (1). 2. keep warm / indoors (1). 3. less energy lost as heat / movement (1). 4. more energy for growth (1). 5. high protein / calorie food (1). 6. antibiotics / hormones (1).
